netective.structure package#

Submodules#

netective.structure.dataviz module#

netective.structure.dataviz.create_symmetric_heatmap(dataframe, title: str, method='ward')#
netective.structure.dataviz.plot_distributions(dist_values)#
netective.structure.dataviz.plot_scalars(data_dict)#

netective.structure.properties module#

class netective.structure.properties.AverageDegreeNearestNeighbors(G: Graph)#

Bases: _Property

Average Degree of Nearest Neighbors.

The Average Degree of Nearest Neighbors here is considered for an undirected network, meaning the neighborhood for each node is all nodes it has a connection with, regardless of direction.

compute()#

Compute the average degree of nearest neighbors.

norm_biol()#

NO IMPLEMENTATION.

norm_network()#

Normalize the average degree of nearest neighbors to all nodes in the graph.

CLASS_NAME = 'Average Degree for Nearest Neighbors (Undirected)'#
compute() → array#

Compute the density of the graph.

Returns:

Density of the graph.

Return type:

float

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.AverageLocalEfficiency(G: Graph, **kwargs)#

Bases: _Property

Average Local Efficiency.

Average Local Efficiency is a computed using the giant component of the graph. The average local efficiency of a graph is the average of the local efficiency of each node in the graph. The local efficiency of a node is the global efficiency computed over the neighborhood of the node.

CLASS_NAME = 'Average Local Efficiency'#
compute() → float#

Compute the average local efficiency for each node in the graph.

Returns:

Average local efficiency for each node in the graph.

Return type:

float

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.AverageOutDegreeNearestNeighbors(G: DiGraph)#

Bases: _Property

Average Out-Degree of Nearest Neighbors

Average out-degree of nearest neighbors is defined as de average of out-degrees of each memeber of a node’s neighborhood. In this case, the neighborhood of each node is the list of its successors.

compute()#

Compute the average out-degree for each node in the graph.

norm_biol()#

Normalize the average out-degree of nearest neighbors to all nodes in the graph AND eliminate uninformative 0s.

norm_network()#

Normalize the average out-degree of nearest neighbors to all nodes in the graph.

CLASS_NAME = 'Average Degree for Nearest Neighbors (Out-Out)'#
compute() → array#

Compute the average out-degree of nearest neighbors using Networkx implementation.

Returns:

average out-degree for each node in the graph.

Return type:

np.array

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.AverageShortestPathLength(G: Graph, **kwargs)#

Bases: _Property

Average shortest path length of the graph.

compute()#

Compute the average shortest path length of the graph using the giant component.

norm_biol()#

Normalize the average shortest path length of the graph to the number of parents.

norm_network()#

Normalize the average shortest path length of the graph to the number of nodes.

CLASS_NAME = 'Average Shortest Path Length'#
compute() → int#

Compute the average shortest path length of the graph using the giant component.

Returns:

Average shortest path length of the graph.

Return type:

int

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.BetweennessCentrality(G: Graph, **kwargs)#

Bases: _Property

Betweenness Centrality.

Betweenness centrality is a computed using the giant component of the graph. A high betweenness centrality indicates that a node is a bridge between different parts of the network.

CLASS_NAME = 'Betweenness Centrality'#
compute() → array#

Compute the betweenness centrality for each node in the graph.

Returns:

Betweenness centrality for each node in the graph.

Return type:

np.array

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.Center(G: Graph, **kwargs)#

Bases: _Property

Center.

Center is a computed using the giant component of the graph. The center of a graph is the set of nodes with eccentricity equal to the radius. This class returns the number of nodes in the center.

CLASS_NAME = 'Center'#
compute() → int#

Compute the center for each node in the graph.

Returns:

Center for each node in the graph.

Return type:

int

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.ClusteringCoefficient(G: Graph)#

Bases: _Property

Clustering coefficient of the graph.

compute()#

Compute the clustering coefficient of the graph.

CLASS_NAME = 'Clustering Coefficient'#
compute() → float#
norm_biol() → float#

Direction would be required.

norm_network() → float#

Coeffients are considered already normalized.

class netective.structure.properties.ComplexFeedForwardCircuits(G: DiGraph)#

Bases: _Property

Number of complex feed-forward circuits.

compute()#

Compute the number of complex feed-forward circuits.

norm_biol()#

Normalize the number of complex feed-forward circuits to the number of parents.

norm_network()#

Normalize the number of complex feed-forward circuits to the number of nodes.

CLASS_NAME = 'Complex Feed-Forward Circuits'#
compute() → int#
norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.Degree(G: Graph)#

Bases: _Property

Degree of the graph.

The degree of each node is defined as the number of connections it has.

compute()#

Compute the connectivity of the graph.

norm_biol()#

Not implemented.

norm_network()#

Normalize the connectivity of the graph to the number of nodes.

CLASS_NAME = 'Degree'#
compute() → array#

Compute the degree of the graph.

Returns:

Array with degrees of every node in graph.

Return type:

nparray

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.Density(G: DiGraph)#

Bases: _Property

Density of the graph.

The density of a graph is defined as the ratio of the number of edges to the number of possible edges. The number of possible edges for directed network with self loops is given by n**2, where n is the number of nodes in the graph.

compute()#

Compute the density of the graph.

norm_biol()#

Normalize the density of the graph to the number of parents.

norm_network()#

Normalize the density of the graph to the number of nodes.

CLASS_NAME = 'Density'#
compute() → float#

Compute the density of the graph.

Returns:

Density of the graph.

Return type:

float

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.Diameter(G: Graph, **kwargs)#

Bases: _Property

Diameter of the graph.

compute()#

Compute the diameter of the graph using the giant component.

norm_biol()#

Normalize the diameter of the graph to the number of parents.

norm_network()#

Normalize the diameter of the graph to the number of nodes.

CLASS_NAME = 'Diameter'#
compute() → int#
norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.Eccentricity(G: Graph, **kwargs)#

Bases: _Property

Eccentricity.

Eccentricity is a computed using the giant component of the graph. The eccentricity of a node is the largest shortest distance between that node and any other node in the graph.

CLASS_NAME = 'Eccentricity'#
compute() → array#

Compute the eccentricity for each node in the graph.

Returns:

Eccentricity for each node in the graph.

Return type:

np.array

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
exception netective.structure.properties.EmptyGraphError#

Bases: Exception

Exception raised for empty graph. Nodes with no edges.

class netective.structure.properties.EntropyPK(G: Graph)#

Bases: _Property

Entropy of degree distribution.

Entropy of the degree distribution is defined as the absolute valueof the sum of each probability to have a certain degree multiplied by its own log2.

compute()#

Compute the entropy of the degree distribution for a graph.

norm_biol()#

Not implemented.

norm_network()#

Normalize the entropy of the degree of distribution to the max theoretical value.

CLASS_NAME = 'Entropy of Degree Distribution'#
compute() → float#

Compute the entropy for the degree distribution of the graph.

Returns:

entropy of the degree distribution.

Return type:

float

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.EntropyPKout(G: DiGraph)#

Bases: _Property

Entropy of out-degree distribution.

Entropy of the out-degree distribution is defined as the absolute value of the sum of each probability to have a certain out-degree multiplied by its own log2.

compute()#

Compute the entropy of the out-degree distribution for a graph.

norm_biol()#

Normalize the entropy of the out-degree distribution to the max theoretical value.

norm_network()#

Normalize the entropy of the out-degree of distribution to the max theoretical value.

CLASS_NAME = 'Entropy of Out-Degree Distribution'#
compute() → float#

Compute the entropy for the out-degree distribution of the graph.

Returns:

entropy of the out-degree distribution.

Return type:

float

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#

norm_biol()#

class netective.structure.properties.GenesintheGiantComponent(G: Graph)#

Bases: _Property

Number of genes in the giant component.

compute()#

Compute the number of genes in the giant component.

norm_biol()#

Normalize the number of genes in the giant component to the number of parents.

norm_network()#

Normalize the number of genes in the giant component to the number of nodes.

CLASS_NAME = 'Gene % in the Giant Component'#
compute() → int#
norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.GiniIndex(G: DiGraph)#

Bases: _Property

Gini Index.

Measurement that reflects the inequiality of distribution of resources between entities. For networks, connections are considered as resources and each node is an individual entity. Therefore, we calculate how well (Gini Index = 0) or how unequal (Gini Index = 1) the distribution of links between nodes in the network is. It is calculated thorugh the area under the curve of The Lorenz Curve, which is drawn by the cummulative percentage of total connections which a certain fraction of nodes can have.

This is the computation for Gini Index for directed networks

compute()#

Compute the gini index for the graph.

norm_biol()#

Normalize gini index to consider distribution of resources only between regulators.

norm_network()#

Already normalized.

CLASS_NAME = 'Gini Index'#
compute() → float#

Compute the gini index for the graph.

Returns:

gini index of the entire graph.

Return type:

float

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.GlobalEfficiency(G: Graph, **kwargs)#

Bases: _Property

Global efficiency of a graph.

The global efficiency of a graph is the average of the inverse of the shortest paths between all pairs of nodes.

CLASS_NAME = 'Global Efficiency'#
compute() → float#

Compute the global efficiency of the graph.

Returns:

Global efficiency of the graph.

Return type:

float

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.InDegree(G: DiGraph)#

Bases: _Property

In degree of the graph.

The in degree of each node is defined as the number of predecessors it has.

compute()#

Compute the in connectivity of the graph.

norm_biol()#

Normalize the in connectivity of the graph to the number of parents.

norm_network()#

Normalize the in connectivity of the graph to the number of nodes.

CLASS_NAME = 'In-Degree'#
compute() → array#

Compute the in-degree of the graph.

Returns:

Array with in-degrees of every node in graph.

Return type:

nparray

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.LocalityIndex(G: Graph)#

Bases: _Property

Locality Index.

Measurement that reflects the internality of every connection of all neighbors immediate to each node. It is calculated, for each node, as the number of links between that node’s neighbors (including itself if there is a self-loop) divided by the total number of edges each of those neighbors is involved in.

compute()#

Compute the locality index for every node in the graph.

norm_biol()#

NO IMPLEMENTATION.

norm_network()#

Normalize locality index for every node. Already normalized.

CLASS_NAME = 'Locality Index'#
compute() → array#

Compute the locality index for every node in the graph.

Returns:

locality index for every node.

Return type:

nparray

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#

compute()#

Compute the maximum degree of the graph.

class netective.structure.properties.NumberArcs(G: DiGraph)#

Bases: _Property

Number of arcs in the graph. An arc is defined as an edge in a directed graph,

considering an edge (u,v) separately from the edge (v,u).

compute()#

Compute the number of arcs in the graph.

norm_biol()#

Normalize the number of arcs of the graph to the number of parents.

norm_network()#

Normalize the number of arcs of the graph to the number of nodes.

CLASS_NAME = 'Number of Arcs'#
compute() → int#

Compute the number of arcs in the graph.

Returns:

number of arcs in the graph.

Return type:

int

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#

compute()#

norm_biol()#

Not implemented.

norm_network()#

Normalize the number of edges of the graph to the number of nodes.

CLASS_NAME = 'Number of Edges'#
compute() → int#

Compute the number of arcs in the graph.

Returns:

number of edges in the graph.

Return type:

int

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#

Out degree of the graph.

The out degree of each node is defined as the number of successors it has.

Normalize the out connectivity of the graph to the number of nodes.

Normalize the out connectivity of the graph to the number of nodes.

nparray

class netective.structure.properties.Periphery(G: Graph, **kwargs)#

Bases: _Property

Periphery.

Periphery is a computed using the giant component of the graph. The periphery of a graph is the set of nodes with eccentricity equal to the diameter. This class returns the number of nodes in the periphery.

CLASS_NAME = 'Periphery'#
compute() → int#

Compute the periphery for each node in the graph.

Returns:

Periphery for each node in the graph.

Return type:

int

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.Radius(G: Graph, **kwargs)#

Bases: _Property

Radius.

Radius is a computed using the giant component of the graph. The radius of a graph is the minimum eccentricity in the graph.

CLASS_NAME = 'Radius'#
compute() → int#

Compute the radius of the graph.

Returns:

Radius of the graph.

Return type:

int

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#

Bases: _Property

compute()#

norm_biol()#

norm_network()#

Normalize the number of regulators of the graph to the number of nodes.

compute() → int#

Returns:

int

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.RichClub(G: Graph)#

Bases: _Property

Rich Club Coefficient.

The Rich Club Coefficient for every degree in the graph is defined as the clustering coeffficient of nodes with a higher degree than the degree being evaluated.

compute()#

Compute the rich club coefficient of the graph.

norm_biol()#

NO IMPLEMENTATION.

norm_network()#

NO IMPLEMENTATION.

CLASS_NAME = 'Rich Club Coefficient'#
compute() → array#

Compute the Rich Club Coefficient.

Returns:

distribution of rich club coefficients, by degree in graph.

Return type:

np.array

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#

Bases: _Property

norm_biol()#

int

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.SelfRegulations(G: DiGraph)#

Bases: _Property

Number of self-loops of the directed graph. These represent self regulators in a biological context.

compute()#

Compute the number of self-loops of the graph.

norm_biol()#

Normalize the number of self-loops of the graph to the number of parents.

norm_network()#

Normalize the number of self-loops of the graph to the number of nodes.

CLASS_NAME = 'Self Regulations'#
compute() → int#

Compute the number of self-loops of the graph.

Returns:

Number of self-loops of the graph.

Return type:

int

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.SubgraphCentrality(G: Graph)#

Bases: _Property

Subgraph centrality.

Subgraph centrality is defined as the “sum” of closed walks of different lengths throught the network starting and ending in each node.

compute()#

Compute the subgraph centrality for every node in the graph.

norm_biol()#

NO IMPLEMENTATION.

norm_network()#

Normalize subgraph centrality for every node to the max theoretical value.

CLASS_NAME = 'Subgraph Centrality'#
compute() → array#

Compute the subgraph centrality for every node in the graph.

Returns:

subgraph centrality for every node.

Return type:

nparray

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.UndirDensity(G: Graph)#

Bases: _Property

Density of an undirected graph.

The density of a graph is defined as the ratio of the number of edges to the number of possible edges. The number of possible edges for directed network with self loops is given by n**2, where n is the number of nodes in the graph.

compute()#

Compute the density of the graph.

norm_biol()#

Normalize the density of the graph to the number of parents.

norm_network()#

Normalize the density of the graph to the number of nodes.

CLASS_NAME = 'Undirected Density'#
compute() → float#

Compute the density of the graph.

Returns:

Density of the graph.

Return type:

float

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#
class netective.structure.properties.UndirGiniIndex(G: Graph)#

Bases: _Property

Undirected Gini Index.

Measurement that reflects the inequiality of distribution of resources between entities. For networks, connections are considered as resources and each node is an individual entity. Therefore, we calculate how well (Gini Index = 0) or how unequal (Gini Index = 1) the distribution of links between nodes in the network is. It is calculated thorugh the area under the curve of The Lorenz Curve, which is drawn by the cummulative percentage of total connections which a certain fraction of nodes can have.

This is the computation for Gini Index for undirected networks.

compute()#

Compute the gini index for the graph.

norm_biol()#

Not implemented.

norm_network()#

Already normalized.

CLASS_NAME = 'Undirected Gini Index'#
compute() → float#

Compute the gini index for the graph.

Returns:

gini index of the entire graph.

Return type:

float

norm_biol(*args)#
norm_network(*args)#

Decorator to check if raw value is None. If it is, raise an error.

netective.structure.structure module#

class netective.structure.structure.GraphObserver(G: Graph | DiGraph, data: bool = False)#

Bases: object

A class to observe changes in a graph.

changed(G: Graph | DiGraph = None, update_G: bool = False) → bool#

Check if G has changed with reference to the last call.

Parameters:

  • G (nx.Graph | nx.DiGraph) – The graph to check. If None, the original graph will be used.

  • update_G (bool) – If True, the graph will be updated to G. If False, the graph will not be updated.

Returns:

True if the graph has changed, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

Raises:

ValueError – If G is None and update_G is True.

Note

If G is None and update_G is False, the original graph will be used (default behavior).

The following table shows the behavior of the function: hash_org_G: hash of the original graph hash_new_G: hash of the new graph

G | update_G | return | update graph | update hash |

|---|———-|--------|————–|-------------| | None | False | hash_org_G == new_hash_org_G | False | True | | not None | True | hash_org_G == hash_new_G | True | True | | not None | False | hash_org_G == hash_new_G | False | False | | None | True | ValueError | False | False |

class netective.structure.structure.NormObserver(norm)#

Bases: object

A class to observe changes in the normalization strategy.

change()#

Check if norm has changed with reference to the last call.

Returns:

True if the norm has changed, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

class netective.structure.structure.Structure(G: DiGraph | Graph, norm: None | str | pd.Series = None, net_id: str = None, verbose: bool = False)#

Bases: object

get_props(props: str | list = 'all', child_classes: list = None) → Tuple[dict[str, dict], dict[str, dict]]#

Computes the structural properties of a network. Either props or child_classes must be provided. If both are provided, props is ignored.

Parameters:

  • props – str|list. Structural properties to compute. Use ‘all’ to compute all the properties. Use a list of property names to compute only those properties. Use a list of property classes to compute all the properties of those classes.

  • child_classes – dict. List of property classes to compute all the properties of those classes.

Returns:

If keep_names is True, it returns a dictionary with the name_id as key and as its value, a dict with property names as keys. Otherwise, it returns a flattened array with the values.

property norm: None | str | pd.Series#

Normalization factor for each property.

Returns:

Normalization factor for each property.

Return type:

None|str|pd.Series

netective.structure.structure.characterize_network(G: DiGraph | Graph, name: str, norm: str | None = None, selected_props: str | list = 'all', child_classes: dict = None, verbose: bool = False, return_prop_dicts: bool = False) → None | Tuple[dict, dict]#

Module-level function to characterize a single network.

Parameters:

  • G (DiGraph | Graph) – Network to characterize.

  • norm (str, optional) – Normalization to apply. Valid values are ‘network’, ‘biological’ or None. Defaults to None.

  • selected_props (str | list, optional) – Properties to compute. Defaults to ‘all’ (all properties).

  • child_classes (dict, optional) – Dict of child classes to compute. Defaults to None. Use either selected_props or child_classes. if child_classes is not None, selected_props is ignored.

  • verbose (bool, optional) – If True, print messages. Defaults to False.

Returns:

Dictionary with the properties of the network if return_prop_dicts is True. None: If return_prop_dicts is False. The figures are shown.

Return type:

dict

Raises:

Exception – Raised if the normalization is not valid.

netective.structure.structure.common_props_dict(networks)#
netective.structure.structure.compare_structure(networks: dict, norm: str | None = None, selected_props: str | list = 'all', workers: str | int = 'auto', return_prop_dicts: bool = False, association_metric: Callable = <function pearsonr>, verbose: bool = False) → Tuple[dict, dict] | plt.Figure#

Module-level function to compare multiple networks.

Returns a tuple of figures, one for the scalar properties and one for the distributions if return_prop_dicts is False. Otherwise, it returns a tuple of dictionaries, one for the scalar properties and one for the distributions.

Parameters:

  • networks (dict) – Dictionary of networks to compare {‘name’:DiGraph | Graph}.

  • norm (str, optional) – Normalization to apply. Valid values are ‘network’, ‘biological’ or None. Defaults to None.

  • selected_props (str | list, optional) – Properties to compute. Defaults to ‘all’ (all properties).

  • workers (int, optional) – Number of workers to use. Defaults to ‘auto’.

Raises:

  • NormalizationError – Raised if the normalization is not valid.

  • ValueError – Raised if there is not enough data to compare.

netective.structure.structure.flatten_list_of_iterables(lst)#
netective.structure.structure.get_child_classes(parent_class, selected_props) → dict#
netective.structure.structure.save_strucs(scalar_props: dict, dist_props: dict, output_dir: str = 'C:\\Users\\jmere\\Dropbox (FreyreLab)\\netective\\docs', delimiter: str = '\t', cl: str | None = None) → None#

Save the structural properties in a file containing the name of the network and the properties.

Parameters:

  • scalar_props – dict. Dictionary with the scalar properties of the networks. {network_name: {property_name: property_value}}.

  • dist_props – dict. Dictionary with the distribution properties of the networks. {network_name: {property_name: property_value}}.

  • output_dir – str. Path to the output directory.

  • delimiter – str. Delimiter to use in the output file.

  • cl – str. Command line used to run the script.

Returns:

None.

netective.structure.structure.struc_props_call(G: DiGraph | Graph, net_id: str, norm: None | str | pd.Series, erdos_renyi: int, verbose: bool = False)#

Call the function struc_props with erdos_renyi random graphs with the same number of nodes and edges as G.

Parameters:

  • G – DiGraph or Graph. Network to compute the structural properties.

  • net_id – str. Name of the network.

  • norm – bool. If True, the properties are normalized (biological criteria).

  • erdos_renyi – int. Number of random graphs to generate with the same number of nodes and edges as G. If 0, only the properties of G are computed. If greater than 0, the properties of G and the average properties of the random graphs are computed.

Returns:

list of tuples with the network id and the properties of the network.

Return type:

list(tuple(str, dict))

Raises:

ValueError – if erdos_renyi is less than 0.
